philosophy.core: Richard's Slack channel list IS the source of truth. Call list_channels each cycle — that's what to scan. The registry defines HOW to scan and WHERE to search beyond the channel list. No static channel lists. No artificial caps. Ingest everything, synthesize ruthlessly.

two_layers.layer_1_channel_ingestion: Read every channel Richard is in. Depth determined by sidebar section. list_channels is called fresh each cycle — if Richard joins or leaves a channel, the system adapts automatically.
two_layers.layer_2_proactive_search: Search BEYOND Richard's channel list. Find mentions of him in channels he's not in. Find discussions about his projects in channels he doesn't know about. Discover channels he should join. This is where the dynamic thinking framework lives.

channel_ingestion.source: list_channels (called fresh each cycle — channelTypes: public_and_private, dm, group_dm)

section_rules.description: Richard's sidebar sections determine ingestion depth. The agent reads the section name from list_channels and applies the matching rule. If a channel has no section or an unknown section, default to 'standard'.

WW Testing.description: Core working channels for worldwide testing workstream. Full ingestion: all messages + all threads + reaction checks on Richard-tagged messages.

AB PS.description: Core paid search team channels. Full ingestion: all messages + all threads + reaction checks. This is where the team works day-to-day.
AB PS.five_levels: L1-L2 — daily execution and testing coordination

AB.description: Broader Amazon Business org channels. Read all messages, pull threads selectively (3+ replies, Richard-tagged, People Watch authors). Good for org awareness, strategic signals, cross-team coordination.
AB.five_levels: L1-L2 — visibility, org awareness, stakeholder signals

AI.description: AI and agent development channels. Read all messages, pull threads selectively. Rich with Level 3-5 signals: tool adoption, agent patterns, platform updates.

Channels.description: General interest, announcements, personal channels. Skim for anything that mentions Richard, paid search, or Amazon Business. Don't pull threads unless directly relevant.

dm_rules.description: DMs and group DMs are where candid coordination happens. The API returns ALL DMs (50+ with pagination), not just the sidebar top 10. Scan any DM with new messages since last scan — read or unread doesn't matter. The system needs the context regardless of whether Richard already saw it.
scanning_logic: 1. Call list_channels with channelTypes=['dm'] and limit=100. Paginate if next_cursor exists.
2. Call list_channels with channelTypes=['group_dm'] and limit=100.
3. For each DM: pull messages since last scan timestamp (from scan state). If new messages exist, ingest them.
4. Skip DMs with is_user_deleted=true and no new messages.
5. Full ingestion on any DM with new messages — all messages + threads since last scan.

full.reactions: Check reactions on messages tagging Richard
full.synthesis: Full narrative synthesis — what happened, what's resolved, what's open

standard.threads: Threads with 3+ replies, threads tagging Richard, threads from People Watch authors
standard.reactions: Check reactions on messages tagging Richard
standard.synthesis: Signal extraction — decisions, action items, status changes, mentions

light.messages: ALL messages since last scan (skim for relevance)
light.threads: Only threads that directly tag Richard or mention paid search / Amazon Business
light.synthesis: Flag only if directly relevant to Richard's work

channel_ingestion.unread_optimization: list_channels returns unread counts and last_read timestamps. Use these to prioritize: channels with unreads get scanned first. Channels with mention_count > 0 get highest priority. Use last_read as the 'oldest' parameter for batch_get_conversation_history — this naturally scopes to what Richard hasn't seen yet.
process: 1. Call list_channels (public_and_private) — get all channels with sections, unread counts, last_read timestamps
3. Call list_channels (group_dm) — get all group DMs
4. Sort by priority: mention_count > 0 first, then unreads, then by section (WW Testing > AB PS > AB > AI > Channels)
5. For each channel, apply the depth rule from its section
6. batch_get_conversation_history using last_read as oldest (or last_scan_ts from scan state, whichever is older — catch messages Richard read but the system hasn't processed)
7. For threads: batch_get_thread_replies per depth rules
8. For Richard-tagged messages: reaction_tool to check acknowledgment
10. No caps on messages, threads, or signals. Read everything the depth rule says to read.

proactive_search.description: Layer 2: search BEYOND Richard's channel list. This is where the agent finds signals in channels Richard isn't in, discovers new channels, and catches cross-org discussions about his work. The channel ingestion handles everything Richard has joined. Proactive search handles everything else.

permanent.description: Always run. These catch signals that channel ingestion can't — mentions of Richard in channels he's not in.

queries.reason: Direct alias mention anywhere on Slack — channels Richard isn't in, cross-org threads
Full name mention — catches references where alias isn't used
Manager messages in channels Richard isn't in — Brandon is in more channels than Richard
Skip-level director — strategic signals in leadership channels Richard can't see

permanent.notes: These overlap with channel ingestion for channels Richard IS in — that's fine. The dedup rule handles it. The value is catching what's OUTSIDE his channel list.

dynamic.description: Agent constructs fresh each cycle based on today's context. These go beyond Richard's channels to find signals he wouldn't otherwise see.
inputs_to_read: current.md — active projects, pending actions, who Richard owes responses to
Today's calendar — who is Richard meeting? What topics?
Channel ingestion results — what topics came up that might have broader discussion elsewhere?
query_philosophy: DEFAULT TO BROAD. You're searching outside Richard's channels — cast wide.
PEOPLE SEARCHES find activity in channels Richard isn't in. from:@alias catches everything that person said anywhere.
TOPIC SEARCHES find discussions Richard doesn't know about. 'paid search' in a channel Richard isn't in could surface a reorg, a new tool, or someone asking about his team.
DISCOVERY SEARCHES find channels Richard should join. If a topic keeps surfacing in a channel he's not in, flag it.
LEVEL 3-5 SEARCHES mine community channels for automation patterns, AEO/AI Overviews discussions, agent orchestration insights, tool adoption signals.
No caps. Run as many queries as the context demands.
thinking_framework: WHO is talking about Richard's work outside his channels? Search for teammates and stakeholders who are in channels Richard isn't.
WHAT projects have cross-org visibility? Search for project names that might be discussed in leadership channels, cross-team channels, or community channels.

WHAT's happening in the Level 3-5 space? Search community channels for: tool adoption patterns, AI wins that could inspire Richard's team, agent architectures, Kiro/AgentSpaces updates, AEO/zero-click discussions.
WHAT channels should Richard join? If a search consistently surfaces relevant signals from a channel Richard isn't in, flag it as a join recommendation.
WHAT did channel ingestion surface that needs broader context? If a topic came up in a team channel, search for the same topic across all of Slack to get the full picture.

reaction_checking.description: Richard acknowledges messages with reactions instead of text replies. A reaction IS a response.
reaction_checking.when_to_check: Any message tagging Richard that appears to request action or a response.

semantics.any_reaction: Any reaction from U040ECP305S means he saw the message. Downgrade urgency.

impact: confirmed_done reaction → not unanswered, lower urgency
acknowledged reaction → seen but may still need action, moderate urgency
any reaction → downgrade from 'X days stale' to 'acknowledged via reaction'
no reaction AND no reply → flag as unanswered, full urgency
reaction_checking.thread_context: Also check if Richard replied in the thread, replied in a different thread on the same topic, or if someone else resolved the ask. Slack conversations are non-linear.

synthesis.description: The output stage. All the ingestion and search data gets compressed into actionable signals. No word caps. No signal caps. Write as much as the data warrants.
digest_rules: Produce a Slack digest in intake/ only if there are actionable signals.
No word cap. If there are 20 signals, write 20 signals. Truncating input is worse than a longer digest.
Prioritize: action items for Richard > decisions that affect Richard > status changes on his projects > FYI signals > Level 3-5 insights.
[ACTION-RW] prefix on anything requiring Richard's direct action.
Include source attribution: channel, author, timestamp, thread link.
For Level 3-5 signals from community channels, include a 'LEVEL 3-5 INSIGHTS' section — what's happening in the ecosystem that Richard should know about for his growth trajectory.
Flag channel join recommendations if proactive search consistently surfaces relevant signals from channels Richard isn't in.
